disclaimer: Prototype disclaimer: This screen reflects a manual March 25, 2026 market snapshot, but it is not validated for trading, investment, or risk decisions. It would need to be hooked up to real-time market and news sources, then scored by tested algorithms and monitoring before production use.

topThemes.impact: Equities, oil, yields, broad risk appetite

rateCurveRead: As of the March 25 snapshot, Treasury yields eased as oil fell and markets responded positively to guarded hopes of a ceasefire proposal, though rates remain elevated versus pre-war levels.

crossMarket.trend: Leading on lower oil and rates

drivers.name: Ceasefire optimism
drivers.detail: A U.S. 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran lifted hopes for de-escalation and drove a broad relief move across global equities.

drivers.name: Oil price reversal lower
drivers.detail: Crude fell as traders priced a lower probability of prolonged supply disruption, reducing some immediate inflation anxiety.

drivers.name: Yields retrace from highs
drivers.detail: Treasury yields moved lower as markets saw less need to price a fresh inflation shock from energy.

drivers.name: Headline whipsaw still alive
drivers.detail: The market remains highly sensitive to contradictory war headlines, which can still reverse leadership quickly.

risks.title: False relief rally
risks.desc: Any denial or breakdown in talks could quickly reverse the bounce in stocks and the drop in oil.

risks.title: Headline-driven volatility
risks.desc: Markets are still trading news flow first, with major reversals possible hour to hour.

risks.title: Rates still restrictive
risks.desc: Even after today’s easing, yields remain high enough to keep financial conditions relatively tight.

risks.title: Inflation pressure not gone
risks.desc: A one-day oil pullback does not fully remove the risk of renewed energy-driven inflation.

timeline.headline: Reports emerged that the U.S. sent Iran a 15-point ceasefire proposal.
timeline.effect: Global risk appetite improved and oil started to fall.

timeline.headline: Wall Street opened higher as traders embraced a possible off-ramp to the war.
timeline.effect: Equities rallied while crude and yields eased.

timeline.headline: Iran said it was reviewing the proposal but denied active negotiations.
timeline.effect: Markets stayed positive but remained jittery.

timeline.headline: Stocks closed higher and oil settled lower on guarded de-escalation hope.
timeline.effect: Risk assets finished the session stronger, though uncertainty remained high.

strategies.title: Trade the Relief, Respect the Fragility
strategies.body: The market is rewarding de-escalation hopes, but conviction should stay tactical because the setup can reverse on a single headline.
strategies.trigger: Positive talks headlines persist and oil stays soft

strategies.title: Prefer Lower-Oil Beneficiaries
strategies.body: Sectors and assets that benefit from easing energy pressure may continue to outperform if crude keeps retreating.

strategies.title: Fade Panic in Yields
strategies.body: If peace hopes continue, markets may keep unwinding the most aggressive higher-for-longer pricing.

strategies.title: Watch for Headline Reversal
strategies.body: A rejection of the proposal or renewed attacks could quickly flip leadership back toward energy and defensives.
